documents.title: Option: hjem.clobberByDefault
documents.content:
The default override behaviour for files managed by Hjem.
While true, existing files will be overriden with new files on rebuild. The behaviour may be modified per-user by setting {option}hjem.users.<username>.clobberFiles to the desired value.

documents.title: Option: hjem.extraModules
documents.content: Additional modules to be evaluated as a part of the users module inside {option}config.hjem.users.<username>. This can be used to extend each user configuration with additional options.

documents.title: Option: hjem.linker
documents.content:
Package to use to link files.
By default, we use smfh, our own file linker.
Setting this to null will use systemd-tmpfiles, which is only supported on Linux.
systemd-tmpfiles is more mature, but it has the downside of leaving behind symlinks that may not get invalidated until the next GC, if an entry is removed from {option}hjem.<user>.files.
Specifying a package will use a custom file linker that uses an internally-generated manifest. The custom file linker must use this manifest to create or remove links as needed, by comparing the manifest of the currently activated system with that of the new system. This prevents dangling symlinks when an entry is removed from {option}hjem.<user>.files.

documents.title: Option: hjem.linkerOptions
documents.content:
Additional arguments to pass to the linker.
This is for external linker modules to set, to allow extending the default set of hjem behaviours. It accepts either a list of strings, which will be passed directly as arguments, or an attribute set, which will be serialized to JSON and passed as --linker-opts options.json.

documents.title: Option: hjem.specialArgs
documents.content: Additional specialArgs are passed to Hjem, allowing extra arguments to be passed down to to all imported modules.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users
documents.content: Hjem-managed user configurations.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.clobberFiles
documents.content:
The default override behaviour for files managed by Hjem for a particular user.
A top level option exists under the Hjem module option {option}hjem.clobberByDefault. Per-file behaviour can be modified with {option}hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.clobber.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.directory
documents.content: The home directory for the user, to which files configured in {option}hjem.users.<username>.files will be relative to by default.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable home management for this user.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.environment.loadEnv
documents.content:
A POSIX compliant shell script containing the user session variables needed to bootstrap the session.
As there is no reliable and agnostic way of setting session variables, Hjem's environment module does nothing by itself. Rather, it provides a POSIX compliant shell script that needs to be sourced where needed.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.environment.sessionVariables
documents.content: A set of environment variables used in the user environment. If a list of strings is used, they will be concatenated with colon characters.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.extraDependencies
documents.content:
A list of paths/packages that should be included in the system closure but generally not visible to users.
On NixOS these paths are forwarded to {option}system.extraDependencies. On nix-darwin, which has no equivalent host option, the paths are pinned to the system closure by listing them in /etc/hjem/extra-dependencies.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files
documents.content: Hjem-managed files.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.clobber
documents.content:
Whether to "clobber" existing target paths.
If using the systemd-tmpfiles hook (Linux only), tmpfile rules will be constructed with L+ (recreate) instead of L (create) type while this is set to true.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable creation of this file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.executable
documents.content: Whether to set the execute bit on the target file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.generator
documents.content:
Function that when applied to value will create the source or text of the file.
Detection is automatic, as we check if the generator generates a derivation or a string after applying to value.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.gid
documents.content: Group ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.permissions
documents.content: Permissions (in octal) to set on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.source
documents.content: Path of the source file or directory.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.target
documents.content: Path to target file relative to /home/‹username›.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.text
documents.content: Text of the file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.type
documents.content: Type of path to create.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.uid
documents.content: User ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.files.<path>.value
documents.content: Value passed to the generator.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.packages
documents.content: Packages to install for this user.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable Hjem management of systemd units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.packages
documents.content:
Packages containing systemd user unit files to be linked into {file}~/.config/systemd/user/. Unit files are taken from $pkg/etc/systemd/user/ and $pkg/lib/systemd/user/.
Units declared in {option}systemd.services and friends take precedence over package-provided units with the same name.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths
documents.content: Definition of systemd per-user path units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.after
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay this unit until they have started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.aliases
documents.content: Aliases of that unit.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.before
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.bindsTo
documents.content: Like ‘requires’, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.conflicts
documents.content: If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.description
documents.content: Description of this unit used in systemd messages and progress indicators.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.documentation
documents.content: A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.enable
documents.content: If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. serial-getty@ttyS0) from being started. Note that enable=true does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.name
documents.content: The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.onFailure
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.onSuccess
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "inactive" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.overrideStrategy
documents.content:
Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd:
asDropinIfExists creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf.
asDropin creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service).
See also {manpage}systemd.unit(5).

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.partOf
documents.content: If the specified units are stopped or restarted, then this unit is stopped or restarted as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.pathConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Path] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.path(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.reloadTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be reloaded. If anything but a reload trigger changes in the unit file, the unit will be restarted instead.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.requiredBy
documents.content: Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the wantedBy option description this also creates .requires symlinks automatically.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.requires
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started, and stop this unit when the specified units are stopped or fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.requisite
documents.content: Similar to requires. However if the units listed are not started, they will not be started and the transaction will fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.restartTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be restarted.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.startLimitBurst
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.startLimitIntervalSec
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.unitConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Unit] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.unit(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.upheldBy
documents.content: Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.upholds
documents.content: Keeps the specified running while this unit is running. A continuous version of wants.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.wantedBy
documents.content:
Units that want (i.e. depend on) this unit. The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to ["multi-user.target"] for system services. Likewise for user units (systemd.user.<name>.*) set it to ["default.target"] to make a unit start by default when the user <name> logs on.
This option creates a .wants symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running systemctl enable. The [Install] section described in {manpage}systemd.unit(5) however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.paths.<name>.wants
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services
documents.content: Definition of systemd per-user service units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.after
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay this unit until they have started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.aliases
documents.content: Aliases of that unit.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.before
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.bindsTo
documents.content: Like ‘requires’, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.conflicts
documents.content: If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.description
documents.content: Description of this unit used in systemd messages and progress indicators.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.documentation
documents.content: A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.enable
documents.content: If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. serial-getty@ttyS0) from being started. Note that enable=true does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.enableDefaultPath
documents.content: Whether to append a minimal default {env}PATH environment variable to the service, containing common system utilities.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.enableStrictShellChecks
documents.content:
Enable running shellcheck on the generated scripts for this unit.
When enabled, scripts generated by the unit will be checked with shellcheck and any errors or warnings will cause the build to fail.
This affects all scripts that have been created through the script, reload, preStart, postStart, preStop and postStop options for systemd services. This does not affect command lines passed directly to ExecStart, ExecReload, ExecStartPre, ExecStartPost, ExecStop or ExecStopPost.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.environment
documents.content: Environment variables passed to the service's processes.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.name
documents.content: The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.notSocketActivated
documents.content: If set, a changed unit is never assumed to be socket-activated on configuration switch, even if it might have associated socket units. Instead, the unit will be restarted (or stopped/started) as if it had no associated sockets.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.onFailure
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.onSuccess
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "inactive" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.overrideStrategy
documents.content:
Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd:
asDropinIfExists creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf.
asDropin creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service).
See also {manpage}systemd.unit(5).

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.partOf
documents.content: If the specified units are stopped or restarted, then this unit is stopped or restarted as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.path
documents.content: Packages added to the service's {env}PATH environment variable. Both the {file}bin and {file}sbin subdirectories of each package are added.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.postStart
documents.content: Shell commands executed after the service's main process is started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.postStop
documents.content: Shell commands executed after the service's main process has exited.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.preStart
documents.content: Shell commands executed before the service's main process is started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.preStop
documents.content: Shell commands executed to stop the service.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.reload
documents.content: Shell commands executed when the service's main process is reloaded.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.reloadIfChanged
documents.content:
Whether the service should be reloaded during a NixOS configuration switch if its definition has changed. If enabled, the value of {option}restartIfChanged is ignored.
This option should not be used anymore in favor of {option}reloadTriggers which allows more granular control of when a service is reloaded and when a service is restarted.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.reloadTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be reloaded. If anything but a reload trigger changes in the unit file, the unit will be restarted instead.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.requiredBy
documents.content: Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the wantedBy option description this also creates .requires symlinks automatically.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.requires
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started, and stop this unit when the specified units are stopped or fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.requisite
documents.content: Similar to requires. However if the units listed are not started, they will not be started and the transaction will fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.restartIfChanged
documents.content: Whether the service should be restarted during a NixOS configuration switch if its definition has changed.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.restartTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be restarted.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.script
documents.content: Shell commands executed as the service's main process.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.scriptArgs
documents.content: Arguments passed to the main process script. Can contain specifiers (% placeholders expanded by systemd, see {manpage}systemd.unit(5)).

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.serviceConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Service] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.service(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.startAt
documents.content: Automatically start this unit at the given date/time, which must be in the format described in {manpage}systemd.time(7). This is equivalent to adding a corresponding timer unit with {option}OnCalendar set to the value given here.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.startLimitBurst
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.startLimitIntervalSec
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.stopIfChanged
documents.content: If set, a changed unit is restarted by calling {command}systemctl stop in the old configuration, then {command}systemctl start in the new one. Otherwise, it is restarted in a single step using {command}systemctl restart in the new configuration. The latter is less correct because it runs the ExecStop commands from the new configuration.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.unitConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Unit] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.unit(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.upheldBy
documents.content: Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.upholds
documents.content: Keeps the specified running while this unit is running. A continuous version of wants.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.wantedBy
documents.content:
Units that want (i.e. depend on) this unit. The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to ["multi-user.target"] for system services. Likewise for user units (systemd.user.<name>.*) set it to ["default.target"] to make a unit start by default when the user <name> logs on.
This option creates a .wants symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running systemctl enable. The [Install] section described in {manpage}systemd.unit(5) however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.services.<name>.wants
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started.

Likewise for user units (systemd.user.<name>.*) set it to ["default.target"] to make a unit start by default when the user <name> logs on.
This option creates a .wants symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running systemctl enable. The [Install] section described in {manpage}systemd.unit(5) however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design.

documents.content: A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.enable
documents.content: If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. serial-getty@ttyS0) from being started. Note that enable=true does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.listenDatagrams
documents.content: For each item in this list, a ListenDatagram option in the [Socket] section will be created.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.listenStreams
documents.content: For each item in this list, a ListenStream option in the [Socket] section will be created.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.name
documents.content: The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.onFailure
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.onSuccess
documents.content: A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "inactive" state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.overrideStrategy
documents.content:
Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd:
asDropinIfExists creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf.
asDropin creates a drop-in file named overrides.conf. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service).
See also {manpage}systemd.unit(5).

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.partOf
documents.content: If the specified units are stopped or restarted, then this unit is stopped or restarted as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.reloadTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be reloaded. If anything but a reload trigger changes in the unit file, the unit will be restarted instead.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.requiredBy
documents.content: Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the wantedBy option description this also creates .requires symlinks automatically.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.requires
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started, and stop this unit when the specified units are stopped or fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.requisite
documents.content: Similar to requires. However if the units listed are not started, they will not be started and the transaction will fail.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.restartTriggers
documents.content: An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be restarted.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.socketConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Socket] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.socket(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.startLimitBurst
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.startLimitIntervalSec
documents.content: Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.unitConfig
documents.content: Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the [Unit] section of the unit. See {manpage}systemd.unit(5) for details.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.upheldBy
documents.content: Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.upholds
documents.content: Keeps the specified running while this unit is running. A continuous version of wants.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.wantedBy
documents.content:
Units that want (i.e. depend on) this unit. The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to ["multi-user.target"] for system services. Likewise for user units (systemd.user.<name>.*) set it to ["default.target"] to make a unit start by default when the user <name> logs on.
This option creates a .wants symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running systemctl enable. The [Install] section described in {manpage}systemd.unit(5) however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.sockets.<name>.wants
documents.content: Start the specified units when this unit is started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets
documents.content: Definition of systemd per-user target units.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.after
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay this unit until they have started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.aliases
documents.content: Aliases of that unit.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.before
documents.content: If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.bindsTo
documents.content: Like ‘requires’, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.conflicts
documents.content: If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.description
documents.content: Description of this unit used in systemd messages and progress indicators.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.systemd.targets.<name>.documentation

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.user
documents.content: The owner of a given home directory.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.directory
documents.content:
The XDG cache directory for the user, to which files configured in {option}hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files will be relative to by default.
Adds {env}XDG_CACHE_HOME to {option}environment.sessionVariables for this user if changed.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files
documents.content: Hjem-managed cache files.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.clobber
documents.content:
Whether to "clobber" existing target paths.
If using the systemd-tmpfiles hook (Linux only), tmpfile rules will be constructed with L+ (recreate) instead of L (create) type while this is set to true.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable creation of this file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.executable
documents.content: Whether to set the execute bit on the target file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.generator
documents.content:
Function that when applied to value will create the source or text of the file.
Detection is automatic, as we check if the generator generates a derivation or a string after applying to value.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.gid
documents.content: Group ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.permissions
documents.content: Permissions (in octal) to set on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.source
documents.content: Path of the source file or directory.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.target
documents.content: Path to target file relative to /home/‹username›/.cache.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.text
documents.content: Text of the file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.type
documents.content: Type of path to create.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.uid
documents.content: User ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.cache.files.<path>.value
documents.content: Value passed to the generator.

documents.content:
Function that when applied to value will create the source or text of the file.
Detection is automatic, as we check if the generator generates a derivation or a string after applying to value.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.gid
documents.content: Group ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.permissions
documents.content: Permissions (in octal) to set on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.source
documents.content: Path of the source file or directory.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.target
documents.content: Path to target file relative to /home/‹username›/.local/state.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.text
documents.content: Text of the file.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.type
documents.content: Type of path to create.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.uid
documents.content: User ID to set as owner on the target path.

documents.title: Option: hjem.users.<username>.xdg.state.files.<path>.value
documents.content: Value passed to the generator.
